landing.heroDescription: Capture, replay, and inspect customer sessions with the real Chrome DevTools. Open source. MIT licensed. Self-hostable.
landing.featuresTitle: Built for the way you actually debug
landing.featuresSubtitle: Every primitive a senior engineer reaches for, in one place.
landing.quickStartSubtitle: Drop the SDK in, point your dev console at the dashboard. Done.
landing.ctaSubtitle: Self-host today, or grab the hosted plan when it lands.
landing.heroNote: The demo runs entirely in your browser with seed data — no signup, no backend.
landing.copiedToastDesc: The {{lang}} snippet is ready to paste.
landing.copyFailedToast: Couldn't copy — select the snippet and copy manually.
landing.stat4Label: demo mode · runs in your browser
landing.featuresLead: Replay sessions, debug live tabs, and ship fixes faster — with the real Chrome DevTools, not a cut-down imitation.
landing.featureLiveCaptureBody: Stream Chrome DevTools Protocol events from any page in real time over WebSocket — no installs.
landing.featureReplayBody: rrweb-powered DOM replay with mouse trails, console output, and scrubbable timelines.
landing.featureActivityBody: Sessions, tickets, errors, and joins flow into a chronological feed engineers actually read.
landing.featureSdkBody: Drop in the UMD script tag or ESM import — bundle-size aware, zero runtime dependencies.
landing.featureDevToolsBody: Connect to the same Chromium DevTools you already know — Network, Console, Elements, all of it.
landing.featureSelfHostBody: MIT licensed. Bring your own Postgres, S3, Jira, Slack — or run it offline behind a VPN.
landing.quickStartHeading: Install the SDK, open the dashboard.
landing.quickStartLead: Choose the module, script, or self-host path and start capturing a real session.
landing.ctaHeading: Ready to inspect a real session?
landing.ctaLead: The demo is one click away. Or clone the repo and ship it on your own infrastructure today.
landing.archSubtitle: Explore the internal mechanics and technical implementation of our CDP-based debugging pipeline from client instrumentation to session rendering.
landing.archTabSdkDesc: Injected into the target page to monitor, serialize, and stream user actions and devtools protocol traffic.
landing.archTabSdkBullet1: DOM Mutation Tracking: Uses rrweb v2 to serialize the initial DOM tree and capture layout modifications via MutationObserver.
landing.archTabSdkBullet2: API Hijacking: Hooks into window.fetch, XMLHttpRequest, and console objects to securely intercept execution logs and request payloads.
landing.archTabSdkBullet3: Network Rewrite Engine: Intercepts client-side outgoing network requests to dynamically override headers, bodies, or status codes.
landing.archTabSdkBullet4: Zero Dependencies: Packaged as a lightweight ESM/UMD bundle with no external dependencies to avoid any page performance impact.
landing.archTabGatewayDesc: Performs secure, high-throughput session capture and routes streams to storage pipelines.
landing.archTabGatewayBullet1: Low-latency Streaming: Collects real-time CDP frames and rrweb events using high-speed NestJS WebSockets Gateway.
landing.archTabGatewayBullet2: Rate Limiting & Security: Protects APIs with @nestjs/throttler (100 reqs/60s) and runs structured validation through class-validator.
landing.archTabGatewayBullet3: S3 Archiving: Compresses and exports session logs to AWS S3 once a session ends, keeping the transactional database lean.
landing.archTabGatewayBullet4: Integration Webhook: Dispatches async events to Jira REST API v3 and Slack Bot hooks to automate issue tracking and notifications.
landing.archTabInternalDesc: Manages session states, indexes metadata, and brokers connection protocols for Chromium DevTools.
landing.archTabInternalBullet1: Metadata Indexing: Stores relational session details, tags, team settings, comments, and tickets in PostgreSQL via TypeORM.
landing.archTabInternalBullet2: CDP Protocol Proxy: Acts as a WebSocket broker between the Chromium DevTools UI client and stored session database frames.
landing.archTabInternalBullet3: Dashboard Analytics: Computes weekly/monthly trends, host metrics, and ticket splits to serve visual chart feeds via Recharts.
landing.archTabInternalBullet4: Swagger Documentation: Fully documents and exposes endpoints under Swagger (@ApiTags) for clean self-hosted integration.
landing.archTabClientDesc: Aggregates timelines and launches deep inspection suites for debugging remote systems.
landing.archTabClientBullet1: Synchronized Replay Timeline: Unifies rrweb-player DOM replay viewport with time-aligned console calls and network waterfall charts.
landing.archTabClientBullet2: Embedded DevTools UI: Mounts a custom-compiled Chromium DevTools UI to inspect element trees and payloads natively.
landing.archTabClientBullet3: Multiplayer Collaboration: Implements real-time WebSocket rooms showing presence lists and synced commentary for team debugging.
landing.archTabClientBullet4: Optimized Aesthetics: Built on Radix UI and motion to deliver high-density, keyboard-friendly light/dark control surfaces.

pricing.title: Self-host free,
pricing.subtitle: Use the open-source release on your own infrastructure forever, or join the hosted waiting list.
pricing.planSelfHostedDesc: MIT licensed. Bring your own infrastructure, keep all your data.
pricing.planSelfHostedFootnote: Best for teams with existing platform engineering capacity.
pricing.planStarterDesc: Hosted by us. For small teams getting started with replay.
pricing.planProDesc: For growing teams that need scale, retention, and SSO.
pricing.aboutHostedDescPrefix: The hosted Starter and Pro tiers are not yet live. The open-source release is fully functional today — see
pricing.noMatchesPrefix: No matches. Try a different keyword or reach out to
pricing.faqDemoFreeQ: Is the public demo really free?
pricing.faqDemoFreeA: Yes — the demo at remote-devtools.vercel.app is fully free, no signup. It runs entirely in your browser with seed data; nothing leaves the page.
pricing.faqSelfVsStarterQ: What's the difference between self-hosted and Starter?
pricing.faqSelfVsStarterA: Self-hosted is the full open-source release on your own infrastructure — Postgres, S3, Jira, Slack integrations all work. Starter is the same product hosted by us with managed Postgres and 30-day session retention.
pricing.faqLaunchQ: When will Starter and Pro launch?
pricing.faqLaunchA: Hosted tiers are on the waitlist. The roadmap (LAUNCH.md) tracks the work needed to get there. Self-host is fully usable today.
pricing.faqSwitchQ: Can I switch from self-host to hosted later?
pricing.faqSwitchA: Yes — sessions live in Postgres. Once we ship the hosted plans, an export/import path will be documented.
pricing.faqVpnQ: Does it work behind a VPN / on-premise / air-gapped?
pricing.faqVpnA: Yes. Self-host has no external dependencies (S3, Jira, Slack, Google Sheets are all optional). Run docker compose on a single VM and you're done.
pricing.faqPrivacyA: The SDK captures DOM and CDP events from the pages you opt into. Self-host means the data never leaves your servers. The hosted plan will include DPA, EU data residency, and per-org isolation.
